A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT AN INDUSTRIAL PLANT ON LA HIGHWAY 405 SOUTHEAST OF PLAQUEMINE LOUISIANA. A SMALL AREA OF SIDING AND ROOF WAS PULLED OFF A METAL BUILDING. DAMAGE PATH APPROXIMATELY ONE THIRD OF MILE LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED ESTIMATED AT 105 MPH...UPPER END EF1 SCALE.
TORNADO TOUCH DOWN AT AN INDUSTRIAL PLANT ON HIGHWAY 405. LIGHT TO MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE REPORTED.

SURVEY DETERMINED SPORADIC DAMAGE IN VARIOUS LOCATIONS NEAR FRANKLINTON LIKELY DUE TO A GRAVITY WAVE OR WAKE DEPRESSION LOW PASSAGE BEHIND ACTUAL SQUALL LINE. DAMAGE ON HIGHWAY 16 AT THE STANDARD GRAVEL COMPANY WAS ROOFING MATERIAL PEELED OFF ROOF AND SNAPPED HARDWARD BRANCHES BLOWN TO THE NE. DAMAGE ON HWY 430 NNE OF FRANKLINTON WAS MINOR WITH PEELED SIDING...ROOFING MATERIAL AND INSULATION BLOWN IN A NE DIRECTION FROM A STRUCTURE UNDER RENOVATION. ACCOMPANIED BY WASHINGTON PARISH EM.

WIND DAMAGE TO SEVERAL VEHICLES AND ROOF DAMAGE TO A BUSINESS ALONG HIGHWAY 16.

SURVEY DETERMINED SPORADIC DAMAGE IN VARIOUS LOCATIONS NEAR FRANKLINTON. DAMAGE ON HIGHWAY 16 AT THE STANDARD GRAVEL COMPANY WAS ROOFING MATERIAL PEELED OFF ROOF AND SNAPPED HARDWARD BRANCHES BLOWN TO THE NE. DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO A COUPLE OF HOUSES IN THE HACKLEY COMMUNITY. ONE HOUSE HAD ROOF BLOWN OFF ALONG WITH PORTION OF WALL DAMAGED. ANOTHER HOUSE HAD MAJOR ROOF DAMAGE AND DAMAGE TO SIDING. TIME BASED ON RADAR.
SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED ALONG HIGHWAY 64 ABOUT 2 MILES EAST OF HIGHWAY 964. RECPORT RECEIVED AT 145 PM. EVENT TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH EF-0 INTENSITY WITH WINDS ESTIMATED 65 TO 75 MPH FOR APPROXIMATELY 12 MILES LONG AND 40 YARDS WIDE. MOST DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO TREES SNAPPED OR DOWNED ALONG WITH NUMEROUS FENCES BLOWN DOWN AND PLAYGROUND EQUIPMENT TOPPLED IN TWO NEIGHBORHOODS ON THE WEST SIDE OF ZACHARY. A STRETCH OF TREE DAMAGE WAS ALSO NOTED NEAR THE COMITE RIVER BRIDGE ON HIGHWAY 67 NEAR OLIVE BRANCH. ONE DETACHED CARPORT WAS DESTROYED.
ROOF DAMAGE TO HOMES...NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN IN THE VICINITY OF HIGHWAY 67 NEAR HIGHWAY 959. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

A TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED BY NWS SURVEY. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON PROGRESS ROAD JUST EAST OF INTERSTATE 59...TRAVELED JUST OVER 1 MILE. THE PATH WIDTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 150 YARDS. OVER ONE DOZEN HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOODS WERE SNAPPED...ONE OF WITCH SEVERELY DAMAGED A PICKUP TRUCK. A METAL WORKSHOP BUILDING HAD SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE IN WHICH HALF OF IT WAS REMOVED. A TRAMPOLINE WAS LIFTED AND THROWN APPROXIMATELY 200 YARDS. THE DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT EF-1 INTENSITY. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 95 MPH.
